architectureVision.statement: Enable Internal through an integrated enterprise architecture spanning business, application, data, and technology domains.
drivers: Reduce fragmentation across organizational and technology change.
Make dependencies and ownership explicit across architecture layers.
Improve governance of strategic change and target-state decisions.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Chief Marketing Officer / VP Marketing outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Marketing-Sourced Pipeline for Chief Marketing Officer / VP Marketing by advancing establish closed-loop attribution and reliable demand tracking. through revenue-aligned demand engine.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Head of Customer Support / Customer Operations outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Time to Resolution for Head of Customer Support / Customer Operations by advancing stabilize service operations with unified intake, triage, and case handling. through reliable case execution.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Chief Marketing Officer / VP Marketing outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Marketing-Sourced Pipeline for Chief Marketing Officer / VP Marketing by advancing establish closed-loop attribution and reliable demand tracking. through revenue-aligned demand engine.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Head of Customer Support / Customer Operations outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Time to Resolution for Head of Customer Support / Customer Operations by advancing stabilize service operations with unified intake, triage, and case handling. through reliable case execution.

principles: Business capabilities drive application and technology design.
Information is modeled as a shared enterprise asset.
Technology choices should be standardized unless a domain-specific need justifies variation.
Implementation should be governed against explicit target and transition architectures.

stakeholders.name: Chief Revenue Officer / VP Sales
stakeholders.concern: Executive owner of pipeline creation, forecast accuracy, quota attainment, and sales productivity across new business and expansion motions. Accountable for turning commercial effort into predictable revenue without adding disproportionate headcount.

stakeholders.name: Chief Marketing Officer / VP Marketing
stakeholders.concern: Executive accountable for pipeline generation, brand equity, demand efficiency, and marketing-attributed revenue. Owns budget allocation across channels and must prove ROI while aligning tightly with Sales on revenue outcomes.

stakeholders.name: Head of Customer Support / Customer Operations
stakeholders.concern: Leader accountable for case resolution quality, response times, cost-to-serve, escalations, and retention risk signals across customer-facing service operations.

stakeholders.name: CFO / Legal, Compliance and Security Leadership
stakeholders.concern: Combined economic buyer group responsible for cash control, auditability, contractual exposure, regulatory compliance, and enterprise risk posture. They succeed when the company scales without margin leakage, control failure, or compliance incidents.

stakeholders.name: Chief Product Officer / CTO / Head of IT
stakeholders.concern: Builder-operator group accountable for delivery speed, service reliability, developer productivity, architecture coherence, tooling cost, and technology governance across product engineering and internal systems.

stakeholders.name: CEO / Founders / Board / Investors
stakeholders.concern: Ultimate economic owners responsible for enterprise growth, operating leverage, risk posture, capital efficiency, and strategic optionality. They care less about individual workflows and more about whether the operating model compounds value as the business scales.
